title: Rate Limiter
description: Design a distributed rate limiter middleware that enforces per-user/per-IP request quotas across a fleet of API servers.
requirements: Enforce request rate limits per user ID and per IP
Support multiple algorithms: token bucket, sliding window
Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header when limit exceeded
Configure limits per API endpoint (e.g. /login stricter than /search)
Limits survive individual API server restarts
nonFunctionalReqs: Consistent enforcement — no overshooting limit by >1%
High availability — Redis failure degrades to allow-all, not block-all

components.label: Client
components.javaNote: Any HTTP client. Receives 429 with headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, Retry-After.

components.label: API Gateway
components.javaNote: Entry point. Extracts userId from JWT or falls back to IP. Calls Rate Limiter before routing to upstream service. On 429, short-circuits response immediately.

components.label:
Rate Limiter
Middleware
components.javaNote: Spring WebFilter or Servlet Filter. Builds Redis key: 'rl:{userId}:{endpoint}:{window}'. Executes Lua script atomically against Redis. Returns allow/deny + remaining tokens.

components.label:
Redis
(Token Bucket State)
components.javaNote: Stores token counts with EXPIRE. Token bucket in Redis: INCR key → if count == 1 set EXPIRE (start of window). Lua script ensures atomicity: no race between INCR and EXPIRE. Cluster mode: consistent hash keys to same shard.

components.label: Response Handler
components.javaNote: Sets rate limit headers on every response. 200 path: X-RateLimit-Remaining=n. 429 path: body includes retryAfterSeconds computed from window reset time.

componentBreakdown.heading: Token Bucket Algorithm
componentBreakdown.detail: Each user gets a bucket of N tokens per window. Each request consumes 1 token. Tokens refill at start of next window. Redis INCR is atomic; Lua script prevents race between INCR and EXPIRE.
componentBreakdown.javaImpl:
// Lua script executed atomically in Redis
local key = KEYS[1]
local limit = tonumber(ARGV[1])
local current = redis.call('INCR', key)
if current == 1 then
  redis.call('EXPIRE', key, ARGV[2])  -- window seconds
end
if current > limit then
  return {0, redis.call('TTL', key)}   -- denied, ttl
end
return {1, limit - current}            -- allowed, remaining

componentBreakdown.heading: Distributed Rate Limiting with Redis Cluster
componentBreakdown.detail: In a multi-node setup, all API servers share Redis state. Use Redis Cluster with consistent hashing so the same user's key always hits the same shard. Lua scripts run atomically per shard.

componentBreakdown.heading: Fail-Open Strategy
componentBreakdown.detail: If Redis is unavailable, the limiter should fail open (allow all requests) rather than blocking all traffic. Log the failure and alert on-call. Never fail closed on infrastructure issues.
